DataFlux AI

Turning customer behavior into actionable intelligence

Now Hiring: Mid Data Scientist

San Francisco, CA (Remote-flexible) · Full-time · $115,000 - $155,000 USD

W

e're hiring a Mid Data Scientist to join our Analytics team. You'll own end-to-end data science projects—

from exploratory analysis and experimentation design to model deployment and monitoring. You'll work

closely with product, engineering, and business stakeholders to identify high-impact opportunities and deliver

predictive models and statistical insights that drive business decisions. This role requires 2-5 years of professional

experience building and shipping data science solutions.

A B O U T U S

DataFlux AI is a Series B data analytics platform company that helps mid-market to enterprise businesses extract

deeper insights from their user behavior and transaction data. Founded in 2020, we've grown to serve 200+ clients

across e-commerce, fintech, and SaaS verticals. Our proprietary ML pipelines and statistical frameworks empower

data teams to move faster from experimentation to production insights.

Y O U R R O L E

Design, execute, and analyze A/B tests and statistical experiments to optimize product features and user

engagement, ensuring statistical rigor and proper sample sizing

Build and maintain predictive models using Python (scikit-learn, XGBoost) to forecast user churn, customer

lifetime value, and segment purchasing behavior

Write efficient SQL queries to extract, transform, and aggregate data from our data warehouse; collaborate with

data engineers on ETL optimization

Develop and document statistical analyses to answer business questions; create clear visualizations and
dashboards to communicate findings to non-technical stakeholders

Implement A/B testing frameworks and experimentation workflows; establish statistical best practices across

the analytics team

Monitor model performance in production; identify drift, retrain models, and iterate on feature engineering and

model architecture

Collaborate with product and engineering teams to define success metrics and translate business requirements

into data science projects

Contribute to documentation and knowledge sharing; mentor junior data team members on statistical concepts

and Python best practices

 R E Q U I R E D Q U A L I F I C AT I O N S

2-5 years of professional experience in data science, analytics, or quantitative roles

Strong proficiency in Python (Pandas, NumPy, scikit-learn) and SQL for data manipulation and analysis

Solid understanding of statistics, probability, hypothesis testing, and experimental design

Experience designing and analyzing A/B tests or running statistical experiments in production environments

Portfolio or demonstrated experience shipping ML models or analyses that influenced business outcomes

Comfort working with ambiguity; ability to scope problems independently and collaborate cross-functionally

D E S I R E D Q U A L I F I C AT I O N S

Experience with XGBoost, LightGBM, or ensemble methods

Familiarity with cloud data warehouses (Snowflake, BigQuery, Redshift) and dbt or similar data transformation

tools

Prior experience with A/B testing platforms (Statsig, LaunchDarkly, Optimizely) or experiment lifecycle

management

Knowledge of causal inference methods or observational study design

T O O L S  O F  T H E T R A D E

Python (Pandas, NumPy, scikit-learn), SQL, XGBoost / scikit-learn, Jupyter Notebooks / Python IDE, Snowflake /

BigQuery, Git & Version Control, A/B Testing & Experimentation Frameworks, Statistics & Hypothesis Testing,
Tableau / Looker (Analytics & Visualization), Statsig (Experimentation Platform)

W E O F F E R

Competitive salary + equity package (stock options)

Comprehensive health insurance (medical, dental, vision coverage)

401(k) matching up to 4% of salary

Unlimited PTO + 10 company holidays

Professional development budget ($2,000/year for courses, conferences, certifications)

Flexible work environment (remote-first with optional in-office days)

Mental health support & wellness programs

Parental leave (16 weeks primary, 8 weeks secondary)

T O A P P LY

 Apply through our careers page at dataflux.ai/careers or email careers@dataflux.ai with your resume, cover letter,

and a link to your portfolio or GitHub. Include a brief note on a recent data science project you're proud of.
